title: Cacio e Pepe
description: A Roman classic of bucatini, sharp Pecorino Romano, and a snowstorm of cracked black pepper. Three ingredients, one perfect plate.
author: Marcella Hazan
instructions: Bring a pot of well-salted water to a boil. Cook pasta to one minute shy of al dente.
Toast cracked pepper in a dry skillet over medium heat for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add a ladle of pasta water and the olive oil to the skillet; reduce slightly.
Transfer pasta to the skillet, add Pecorino off heat, and toss vigorously until creamy.
Plate immediately with extra cheese and pepper on top.

title: Chicken Tinga Tacos
description: Smoky shredded chicken in a chipotle-tomato sauce, piled on warm corn tortillas with crema and pickled onions.
author: Diana Kennedy
instructions: Poach chicken thighs in salted water with bay leaf for 20 minutes. Reserve broth.
Blend chipotles, tomatoes, garlic, and 1 cup poaching liquid until smooth.
Sweat the onion in oil until translucent, then add the sauce and simmer 10 minutes.
Shred chicken and fold into the sauce; cook 5 more minutes to thicken.
Warm tortillas, fill, and top with crema, cotija, and pickled onions.

title: Palak Paneer
description: Cubes of fresh paneer simmered in a velvety spiced spinach gravy. Comfort food with weekday-friendly timing.
author: Madhur Jaffrey
instructions: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 90 seconds; shock in ice water and blend smooth.
Sear paneer cubes in ghee until golden on all sides; remove and set aside.
In the same pan, soften onions, then add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes.
Add spinach purée, garam masala, salt; simmer 8 minutes.
Stir in cream and paneer; warm through. Serve with basmati or naan.

title: Salmon Onigirazu
description: A rice-and-nori sandwich layered with miso-glazed salmon, cucumber, and shiso. Lunchbox perfection.
author: Me
instructions: Whisk miso, mirin, and a splash of soy. Brush over salmon and broil 8 minutes.
Place a nori sheet shiny-side down on plastic wrap.
Layer rice, cucumber, salmon, shiso, and another rice layer in the center.
Fold corners of nori into the middle; wrap tightly in plastic and rest 2 minutes.
Slice in half with a wet knife. Serve with extra soy.

title: Greek Lemon Chicken
description: Sheet-pan chicken thighs roasted with potatoes, oregano, and a punchy lemon dressing. Sunday dinner energy.
author: Diane Kochilas
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss potatoes with half the oil, salt, oregano.
Whisk lemon juice, remaining oil, garlic, oregano. Marinate chicken 10 minutes.
Arrange potatoes and chicken on a sheet pan; tuck lemon slices around.
Roast 45–50 minutes, basting once, until skin is deep gold and potatoes are crisp.
Rest 5 minutes; finish with chopped parsley and an extra squeeze of lemon.

title: Pad See Ew
description: Wide rice noodles wok-tossed with Chinese broccoli, egg, and a sweet-savory dark soy glaze.
author: David Thompson
instructions: Heat a wok smoking-hot. Sear protein until just cooked; push to side.
Crack eggs into the bare wok; let set briefly before scrambling.
Add garlic and gai lan stems; stir-fry 1 minute.
Add noodles and sauces. Toss with minimal stirring to get char marks.
Fold in leaves until wilted. Plate immediately with chili vinegar.

title: Coq au Vin Blanc
description: A lighter take on the bistro classic — chicken braised in white wine with leeks, mushrooms, and tarragon.
author: Julia Child
instructions: Render lardons in a Dutch oven; remove and reserve.
Brown chicken pieces in the fat, working in batches. Set aside.
Sweat leeks until soft; add mushrooms and brown.
Deglaze with wine, return chicken and lardons, add stock to nearly cover.
Cover and braise at 325°F for 60 minutes. Finish with tarragon and a knob of butter.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato-pepper sauce with feta and herbs. Skillet to table in twenty minutes.
author: Yotam Ottolenghi
instructions: Sauté onion and pepper in oil until soft, about 6 minutes.
Bloom cumin and paprika in the pan for 30 seconds.
Add tomatoes and a pinch of sugar; simmer 8 minutes.
Make wells; crack eggs in and cover. Cook 5–7 minutes until whites set.
Top with feta, parsley, and a drizzle of oil. Serve with crusty bread.

title: Buttermilk Pancakes
description: Tall, tangy, melt-in-the-pan pancakes. Served with warm maple syrup and a slab of cold butter.
author: Me
instructions: Whisk dry ingredients in a large bowl with 3/4 tsp salt.
Combine buttermilk, eggs, and melted butter in a measuring cup.
Gently fold wet into dry until just combined; lumps are fine.
Cook 1/4-cup portions on a buttered griddle over medium until bubbles pop, then flip.
Hold in a 200°F oven while you finish the rest. Serve hot.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: Creamy carnaroli rice slow-stirred with porcini stock, cremini, and a generous shower of Parmigiano.
author: Marcella Hazan
instructions: Brown cremini in butter until deeply colored; reserve.
Sweat shallot in butter; add rice and toast 90 seconds.
Deglaze with wine; add porcini and their strained liquid.
Add warm stock a ladle at a time, stirring often, for ~20 minutes.
Off heat, beat in cold butter and Parmigiano. Rest 2 minutes; serve loose.

title: Vietnamese Caramel Tofu
description: Crispy tofu cubes glazed in a smoky coconut-caramel sauce with ginger, lime, and chili.
author: Andrea Nguyen
instructions: Press tofu 15 minutes; cube and sear in oil until golden on all sides.
In a clean pan, melt sugar to a deep amber caramel.
Carefully add coconut milk (it will sputter) and stir to dissolve.
Add soy, ginger, lime; reduce 3 minutes. Fold in tofu and chili.
Glaze tofu over high heat 2 minutes. Serve over jasmine rice.

title: Slow-Roasted Carnitas
description: Pork shoulder confit-style in citrus and lard until shreddable, then crisped under the broiler.
author: Roberto Santibañez
instructions: Season pork generously with salt; arrange in a Dutch oven with aromatics.
Add lard and orange halves (squeezed in). Cover and braise at 300°F for 2.5 hours.
Uncover, raise to 425°F; cook 25 minutes until edges are deeply browned.
Lift pork out, shred with forks, and toss with strained pan juices.
Crisp under the broiler 3 minutes. Serve with warm tortillas and salsa verde.

title: Miso-Glazed Eggplant
description: Halved Japanese eggplant scored, broiled, and lacquered with sweet miso glaze. Vegan, weeknight-fast, deeply savory.
author: Nancy Singleton Hachisu
instructions: Halve eggplants and score the flesh in a diamond pattern.
Broil cut-side up for 8 minutes until tender and browning.
Whisk miso, mirin, sake, sugar into a smooth glaze.
Brush thickly over eggplants; broil 3–4 more minutes until bubbling.
Top with sesame and scallion. Serve over short-grain rice.

title: Chana Masala
description: Chickpeas simmered in a tangy tomato-onion gravy with toasted spices. Pantry-friendly comfort.
author: Meera Sodha
instructions: Brown the onion deeply in oil — at least 10 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste; cook 90 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in tomatoes and spices; simmer 10 minutes.
Add chickpeas with 1 cup water; simmer 12 more minutes.
Finish with amchur and a flurry of cilantro.

title: Lemon Ricotta Pasta
description: Bright, creamy, fifteen-minute pasta. Whole-milk ricotta whipped with lemon zest, tossed through hot spaghetti.
author: Me
instructions: Cook pasta in heavily salted water until al dente; reserve 1 cup water.
Whip ricotta with zest, juice, oil, salt, and a splash of pasta water.
Toss pasta with ricotta sauce off heat; loosen with more water as needed.
Finish with Parmigiano, chili flake, more zest. Eat immediately.

title: Banh Mi Meatballs
description: Lemongrass-pork meatballs in a fish-sauce caramel, piled into baguettes with pickled carrot and herbs.
author: Andrea Nguyen
instructions: Mix pork with lemongrass, half the fish sauce, salt, pepper. Form 1-inch balls.
Sear meatballs in oil until deeply browned; remove.
Caramelize sugar in the pan; add remaining fish sauce and 1/4 cup water.
Return meatballs; glaze 2 minutes until lacquered.
Split baguette, layer meatballs, pickles, cilantro, mayo. Press and slice.

title: Fattoush Salad
description: A tumble of summer vegetables with toasted pita, sumac, and a sharp lemon-pomegranate dressing.
author: Yotam Ottolenghi
instructions: Toast pita pieces with olive oil at 400°F until shatter-crisp.
Combine vegetables and herbs in a wide bowl.
Whisk dressing: lemon, pomegranate molasses, oil, sumac, salt.
Toss salad with dressing and pita just before serving.

title: Skillet Cornbread
description: Crackly-edged buttermilk cornbread baked in a screaming-hot cast iron pan. Salty, sweet enough, never cakey.
author: Edna Lewis
instructions: Heat a 10-inch cast iron skillet in a 425°F oven for 15 minutes.
Whisk dry ingredients with 1 tsp salt.
Combine buttermilk, eggs, honey, and most of the butter; fold into dry.
Swirl remaining butter in the hot skillet; pour batter in.
Bake 22–25 minutes until top is deep gold and edges pull from the pan.

title: Ratatouille Confit
description: Slow-baked ribbons of zucchini, eggplant, and tomato over a thyme-perfumed pepper base. Make-ahead, even better day two.
author: Julia Child
instructions: Roast peppers under the broiler until charred; peel, seed, and blend with onion, garlic, oil, salt.
Spread pepper base in a wide baking dish.
Arrange overlapping vegetable slices on top in concentric rings.
Drizzle with oil, scatter thyme, cover with parchment.
Bake at 300°F for 75 minutes; uncover for last 15 to caramelize.

title: Tomato & White Bean Stew
description: Cannellini beans braised with garlic, rosemary, and good olive oil until the broth turns silky. Crusty bread mandatory.
author: Samin Nosrat
instructions: Warm oil with garlic, rosemary, chili over low heat 5 minutes.
Crush tomatoes by hand into the pot; simmer 10 minutes.
Add beans with their liquid; cook 15 minutes until broth thickens.
Smash a few beans against the pot to enrich. Adjust salt.
Serve drowned in good oil, with grilled bread and parsley.

title: Pork Al Pastor
description: Achiote-marinated pork charred under the broiler, layered with charred pineapple and cilantro on warm corn tortillas.
author: Pati Jinich
instructions: Blend achiote, chiles, pineapple juice, garlic, vinegar into a marinade.
Coat pork; refrigerate 4+ hours.
Stack pork on a vertical skewer (or pile on a sheet pan); broil hot until edges char.
Char pineapple slices under the broiler; dice fine.
Slice pork; serve on tortillas with pineapple, onion, cilantro, lime.

title: Soba with Sesame Greens
description: Cold buckwheat noodles with blanched greens, soft tofu, and a creamy black-sesame dressing.
author: Nancy Singleton Hachisu
instructions: Boil soba 4 minutes; drain and rinse cold until firm.
Blanch greens 30 seconds; shock in ice water; squeeze and chop.
Toast sesame; grind with soy, vinegar, a splash of water into a paste.
Plate noodles, drape greens and tofu, spoon dressing over.
Top with sliced scallion and a final drift of toasted sesame.

title: Tom Kha Gai
description: Coconut-galangal soup with chicken, mushrooms, and a citrusy hit of lime leaf. Bracing and rich at once.
author: David Thompson
instructions: Simmer galangal, lime leaves, lemongrass in stock 5 minutes.
Add coconut milk and chicken; poach 10 minutes.
Add mushrooms; simmer 4 more minutes.
Off heat, season with fish sauce and lime juice.
Garnish with cilantro and bird's-eye chili.

title: Chocolate Olive Oil Cake
description: A one-bowl, dairy-free chocolate cake with a fudgy crumb. Fruity olive oil and dark cocoa do all the work.
author: Nigella Lawson
instructions: Whisk dry ingredients in the cake pan itself.
Make three wells; pour in oil, vinegar, vanilla.
Pour warm water over; whisk until smooth.
Bake at 350°F for 35–40 minutes until set in center.
Cool fully; dust with cocoa or top with flaky salt.
